mailbox/Mailbox/get.name: get
mailbox/Mailbox/get.description: Return a representation of the message corresponding to key. If no such message exists, default is returned if the method was called as get() and a KeyError exception is raised if the method was called as __getitem__(). The message is represented as an instance of the appropriate format-specific Message subclass unless a custom message factory was specified when the Mailbox instance was initialized.

mailbox/Mailbox/get_bytes.name: get_bytes
mailbox/Mailbox/get_bytes.description: Return a byte representation of the message corresponding to key, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists. Added in version 3.2.

info.excerpt: Return a byte representation of the message corresponding to key, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists. Added in version 3.2.

mailbox/mbox/get_bytes.name: get_bytes
mailbox/mbox/get_bytes.description: Note: This method has an extra parameter (from_) compared with other classes. The first line of an mbox file entry is the Unix “From “ line. If from_ is False, the first line of the file is dropped.

info.excerpt: Note: This method has an extra parameter (from_) compared with other classes. The first line of an mbox file entry is the Unix “From “ line. If from_ is False, the first line of the file is dropped.

mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_date.name: get_date
mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_date.description: Return the delivery date of the message as a floating-point number representing seconds since the epoch.

info.excerpt: Return the delivery date of the message as a floating-point number representing seconds since the epoch.

mailbox/Mailbox/get_file.name: get_file
mailbox/Mailbox/get_file.description: Return a file-like representation of the message corresponding to key, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists. The file-like object behaves as if open in binary mode. This file should be closed once it is no longer needed. Changed in version 3.2: The file object really is a binary file; previously it was incorrectly returned in text mode. Also, the file-like object now supports the context manager protocol: you can use a with statement to automatically close it. Note Unlike o...

info.excerpt: Return a file-like representation of the message corresponding to key, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists. The file-like object behaves as if open in binary mode. This file should...

mailbox/Maildir/get_file.name: get_file
mailbox/Maildir/get_file.description: Depending upon the host platform, it may not be possible to modify or remove the underlying message while the returned file remains open.

info.excerpt: Depending upon the host platform, it may not be possible to modify or remove the underlying message while the returned file remains open.

mailbox/mbox/get_file.name: get_file
mailbox/mbox/get_file.description: Using the file after calling flush() or close() on the mbox instance may yield unpredictable results or raise an exception. Note: This method has an extra parameter (from_) compared with other classes. The first line of an mbox file entry is the Unix “From “ line. If from_ is False, the first line of the file is dropped.

info.excerpt: Using the file after calling flush() or close() on the mbox instance may yield unpredictable results or raise an exception. Note: This method has an extra parameter (from_) compared with other classes...

mailbox/MH/get_file.name: get_file
mailbox/MH/get_file.description: Depending upon the host platform, it may not be possible to remove the underlying message while the returned file remains open.

info.excerpt: Depending upon the host platform, it may not be possible to remove the underlying message while the returned file remains open.

mailbox/Babyl/get_file.name: get_file
mailbox/Babyl/get_file.description: In Babyl mailboxes, the headers of a message are not stored contiguously with the body of the message. To generate a file-like representation, the headers and body are copied together into an io.BytesIO instance, which has an API identical to that of a file. As a result, the file-like object is truly independent of the underlying mailbox but does not save memory compared to a string representation.

info.excerpt: In Babyl mailboxes, the headers of a message are not stored contiguously with the body of the message. To generate a file-like representation, the headers and body are copied together into an io.Bytes...

mailbox/Maildir/get_flags.name: get_flags
mailbox/Maildir/get_flags.description: Return as a string the flags that are set on the message corresponding to key. This is the same as get_message(key).get_flags() but much faster, because it does not open the message file. Use this method when iterating over the keys to determine which messages are interesting to get. If you do have a MaildirMessage object, use its get_flags() method instead, because changes made by the message’s set_flags(), add_flag() and remove_flag() methods are not reflected here until the mailbox’s __set...

mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_flags.name: get_flags
mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_flags.description: Return a string specifying the flags that are currently set. If the message complies with the standard Maildir format, the result is the concatenation in alphabetical order of zero or one occurrence of each of 'D', 'F', 'P', 'R', 'S', and 'T'. The empty string is returned if no flags are set or if “info” contains experimental semantics.

mailbox/mboxMessage/get_flags.name: get_flags
mailbox/mboxMessage/get_flags.description: Return a string specifying the flags that are currently set. If the message complies with the conventional format, the result is the concatenation in the following order of zero or one occurrence of each of 'R', 'O', 'D', 'F', and 'A'.

mailbox/Maildir/get_folder.name: get_folder
mailbox/Maildir/get_folder.description: Return a Maildir instance representing the folder whose name is folder. A NoSuchMailboxError exception is raised if the folder does not exist.

info.excerpt: Return a Maildir instance representing the folder whose name is folder. A NoSuchMailboxError exception is raised if the folder does not exist.

mailbox/mboxMessage/get_from.name: get_from
mailbox/mboxMessage/get_from.description: Return a string representing the “From “ line that marks the start of the message in an mbox mailbox. The leading “From “ and the trailing newline are excluded.

info.excerpt: Return a string representing the “From “ line that marks the start of the message in an mbox mailbox. The leading “From “ and the trailing newline are excluded.

mailbox/Maildir/get_info.name: get_info
mailbox/Maildir/get_info.description: Return a string containing the info for the message corresponding to key. This is the same as get_message(key).get_info() but much faster, because it does not open the message file. Use this method when iterating over the keys to determine which messages are interesting to get. If you do have a MaildirMessage object, use its get_info() method instead, because changes made by the message’s set_info() method are not reflected here until the mailbox’s __setitem__() method is called. Added in ver...

mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_info.name: get_info
mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_info.description: Return a string containing the “info” for a message. This is useful for accessing and modifying “info” that is experimental (i.e., not a list of flags).

info.excerpt: Return a string containing the “info” for a message. This is useful for accessing and modifying “info” that is experimental (i.e., not a list of flags).

mailbox/Babyl/get_labels.name: get_labels
mailbox/Babyl/get_labels.description: Return a list of the names of all user-defined labels used in the mailbox. Note The actual messages are inspected to determine which labels exist in the mailbox rather than consulting the list of labels in the Babyl options section, but the Babyl section is updated whenever the mailbox is modified.

mailbox/BabylMessage/get_labels.name: get_labels
mailbox/BabylMessage/get_labels.description: Return a list of labels on the message.

mailbox/Mailbox/get_message.name: get_message
mailbox/Mailbox/get_message.description: Return a representation of the message corresponding to key as an instance of the appropriate format-specific Message subclass, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists.

mailbox/MH/get_sequences.name: get_sequences
mailbox/MH/get_sequences.description: Return a dictionary of sequence names mapped to key lists. If there are no sequences, the empty dictionary is returned.

mailbox/MHMessage/get_sequences.name: get_sequences

mailbox/Mailbox/get_string.description: Return a string representation of the message corresponding to key, or raise a KeyError exception if no such message exists. The message is processed through email.message.Message to convert it to a 7bit clean representation.

mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_subdir.name: get_subdir
mailbox/MaildirMessage/get_subdir.description: Return either “new” (if the message should be stored in the new subdirectory) or “cur” (if the message should be stored in the cur subdirectory). Note A message is typically moved from new to cur after its mailbox has been accessed, whether or not the message has been read. A message msg has been read if "S" in msg.get_flags() is True.

mailbox/BabylMessage/get_visible.name: get_visible
mailbox/BabylMessage/get_visible.description: Return a Message instance whose headers are the message’s visible headers and whose body is empty.

info.excerpt: Return a Message instance whose headers are the message’s visible headers and whose body is empty.
